task_completion.description: Access the CRM Admin panel or Studio
task_completion.visual_evidence: The browser shows the CRM Administration page or the Studio interface.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the fields list for the Accounts module
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Studio interface shows the 'Accounts' module expanded, and the list of fields for Accounts is visible.
The edit view for the 'Industry' (or 'industry') field is open, and the 'Audit' checkbox is checked.
The agent clicks the 'Save' button in the 'Industry' field edit view, or the fields list is shown with an indicator that 'Industry' is now audited.

task_completion.description: Enable auditing for the 'Annual Revenue' field
task_completion.visual_evidence: The edit view for the 'Annual Revenue' (or 'annual_revenue') field is open, and the 'Audit' checkbox is checked.
The agent clicks the 'Save' button in the 'Annual Revenue' field edit view, or the fields list is shown with an indicator that 'Annual Revenue' is now audited.

integrity.description: Agent used the CRM graphical user interface to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and CRM UI, rather than terminal commands or database scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual CRM application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface belongs to a genuine CRM system and not a text editor, mock HTML file, or static image.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing direct database updates (e.g., SQL UPDATE queries) or PHP script executions to modify field definitions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target module is strictly 'Accounts'. The exact fields that must have auditing enabled are 'industry' and 'annual_revenue'. Look for the 'Audit' checkbox being explicitly checked and saved for these two fields in the CRM's Studio/Admin interface.
